****

Damon was naked in a large steaming bathtub in the main room of his residence.

Ah... this life is the best, not even a week since I came here, I already have a harem, though another two are still in progress. A big smile broke out on his face as he let himself sink into the warm water.

Spreading his senses toward the bed, he looked at the two girls on the bed. Looks like both are still passed out.

Suddenly, a soft knock echoed through his house, followed by a soft, graceful voice.

"DAmon, it’s me."

"Ariel? She finally comes?" He stood up with a smile, not even bothering to dry his body.

He just wrapped his body in a bathrobe before going down to the living room on the first floor and opening the door.

Ariel lingered in the doorway, her silk robe damp from the evening’s mist. Her gaze dropped to Damon, then she flung herself against him, and hot tears soaked his chest.

Damon gripped her waist and hugged her.

What happened? She was heartbroken... Is that worthless Jack did something to her?

"Ariel?" He eased back just enough to catch her face, thumb tracing a tear’s path. "Tell me what’s wrong."

She pressed closer, shivering in his embrace. No sounds escaped—only broken sobs buried in his clothing.

He tightened his grip, lifted her, and brought her to the sofa. He sits while Ariel is in his lap, his hands caressing her head, trying to calm her down.

Time passed by as her cry began to soften and change into sobbing.

At last, Ariel pulled away, hands trembling as she gave him a crumpled letter. "R-read... sniff... this."

Damon snatched the paper and read it. His jaw locked as his eyes raked over the ink.

’After you get into the Azure Dragon Sect, yield to Young Master Jack in every circumstance. The Frostborn family sent us a marriage proposal, and we accept that. Consider yourself lucky to receive his attention. You are not fit to be a cultivator, just be a bride.’

Rage surged through his veins. His fingers clenched, crushing the paper’s corner.

He dropped the letter, pulling her tight against his chest. His embrace locked around her like unyielding iron.

"It’s okay. You’re safe by my side. No one will lay a finger on you." His tone remained firm, exhaling, brushing her locks.
